title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple converses with Mrs. Fyne regarding the history of a woman who failed to marry Walter Fyne. After the failed engagement, the woman married a widower and moved to St. Catherine's Villa near the hospital, but she eventually left him for another man. Mrs. Fyne notes that Walter remained friends with the woman, often visiting her and playing with the child from her marriage. That child has since grown up and is now part of the Reed family. Mrs. Fyne also mentions Jackie Avesford, a former office clerk who pursued Helen Kennedy to improve his social status and now runs the Avesford Water Lily Carriage Company. During the conversation, Walter Fyne enters; Miss Marple observes him as a refined, quiet, and somewhat boring man.

Meanwhile, Gwendolyn and Jules visit Anstey Manor, a desolate white house on a barren hill, to investigate Colonel Richard Erskine’s past romantic affairs. Gwendolyn is motivated by her memories of Helen Kennedy. Upon arrival, they meet Colonel Erskine, a man Gwendolyn finds attractive and distinctive, unlike the unremarkable Walter Fyne. They also encounter Mrs. Erskine, Janet, a robust and haggard woman who watches Gwendolyn with suspicion. Gwendolyn engages the Colonel in small talk about real estate and her desire to live in the countryside, while the Colonel describes the area as isolated. He reveals that his family has resided at Anstey Manor for nearly three hundred years, dating back to the reign of Queen Anne.
